title: Grilled Crispy German Beetroot Stir-fry
description: A rich and tender hangover-cure dish featuring raw vegetables grilled to perfection, perfect for breakfast or a light meal.
instructions: Wash the beetroot thoroughly and peel it.
Slice the beetroot into thin rounds or matchsticks. Season with salt on both sides.
Mix almond meal with a little sea salt in a shallow bowl for coating the beetroots.
Brush each beetroot round with olive oil, then coat them lightly with the almond meal mixture.
Preheat your grill to medium-high heat (around 400°F). Grill the beetroot rounds until they develop a nice char and are tender but not falling apart, about 5-10 minutes per side.
While the beetroots are grilling, mix Dijon mustard with lemon zest in a small bowl.
Once the beetroots are done, remove them from the grill and set aside. Add the mustard-lemon mixture to the pan that had been used for grilling the beetroots.
Add minced garlic to the pan, stir-fry briefly until fragrant (do not overcook as it can burn), then add the grilled beetroots back into the pan.
Garnish with fresh herbs if desired and serve hot.
